change_requests.title: Add rate limiting to public API endpoints
change_requests.where: API gateway layer and individual route handlers
change_requests.what: Implement token-bucket rate limiting with configurable thresholds per endpoint
change_requests.how: Use express-rate-limit middleware with Redis-backed store for distributed counting
change_requests.why: Recent load testing revealed unprotected endpoints vulnerable to abuse; compliance requirement for Q1
change_requests.confidence_reason: Explicitly proposed during the call with detailed implementation approach discussed

blockers.description: Production deployment freeze until March 5 prevents schema migration rollout

scope_changes.original_scope: OAuth2 migration covers web application only
scope_changes.new_scope: OAuth2 migration now includes mobile app and CLI tool authentication
scope_changes.reason: Product team requested unified auth experience across all client types
scope_changes.impact: Estimated 1.5 additional sprint points; requires PKCE implementation for public clients

file_references.context: New OAuth2 client implementation with PKCE support

Document rollback test results and share with team

completed_in_call: Reviewed and approved database schema design for multi-tenant support
your_tasks.summary: Primary focus is unblocking the database migration once production freeze lifts on March 5. Meanwhile, validate rollback procedures on staging.

summary: Team discussed migration plan for OAuth2 authentication and multi-tenant database schema. OAuth2 work is in progress with frontend integration underway. Database migration is blocked by production freeze until March 5. Rate limiting was proposed as a new requirement for public API endpoints. Scope of OAuth2 migration expanded to include mobile and CLI clients.
